data: DOW stocks 2018-2022, 3 tiers: demo(5), standard(8), full(12); same as baseline; q=0.5
method: QAOA (p=2 layers, 5 restarts, COBYLA) with cqlib StatevectorSimulator; portfolio QUBO = minimize q·risk − (1−q)·return + selection penalty
optimal_rate: 2/3 (67%) exact optimum at demo(5q) and standard(8q); 125.4% gap at full(12q)
qubo_modeling: H = q·(w^T Σ w) − (1−q)·(μ^T w) + penalty·(Σx_i − k)^2; QUBO normalized
limitations: Simulator-only results (cqlib StatevectorSimulator), NOT real quantum hardware. QAOA finds exact optimum at small-medium scale (5-8 qubits, gap 0%) but shows a LARGE 125.4% gap at 12 qubits (full tier) — this reflects QAOA's known heuristic limitation: with only p=2 layers and 5 restarts, the 2^12-dimensional search landscape is under-explored, and the top-probability bitstring deviates from optimal. Larger p / more restarts would improve but increase cost. Solve time grows sharply (16s→26s→229s). Markowitz (continuous) and QAOA (binary) optimize different formulations, so their returns/risks are not directly comparable; cost_gap is measured against brute-force binary optimum.
